feed.title: Scholar Alerts — Clean Feed
feed.subtitle: Google Scholar alerts reformatted as individual article entries

entry.title: Legged Autonomous Surface Science In Analogue Environments (LASSIE): Making Every Robotic Step Count in Planetary Exploration
entry.content:
Authors: CG Wilson, M Nachon, S Liu, JG Ruck, J Caporale... - arXiv preprint arXiv ..., 2026

Abstract: The ability to efficiently and effectively explore planetary surfaces is currently limited by the capability of wheeled rovers to traverse challenging terrains, and by pre- programmed data acquisition plans with limited in-situ flexibility. In this paper, we ...

Alert: Douglas Jerolmack (author alert)

entry.title: Bioleaching of critical trace metals by Sphingomonas desiccabilis: substrate-driven selectivity in Earth and space analogues
entry.content:
Authors: L Tonietti, M Esposito, M Leggiero, F Bunn, LJ Eades... - Frontiers in Microbiology

Abstract: Biotechnological advances are transforming the potential for sustainable resource utilization in space exploration. Biomining, using microorganisms to extract valuable metals, has emerged as a viable strategy for in situ resource utilization (ISRU) in ...

Alert: Charles Cockell (author alert)

entry.title: Three outstanding physical questions for K2-18 b and other temperate sub-Neptunes
entry.content:
Authors: SM Tsai, P Ferrari, M Kuipers, J Lustig-Yaeger... - arXiv preprint arXiv ..., 2026

Abstract: Recent transmission spectra of the temperate sub-Neptune K2-18 b obtained with JWST have attracted significant attention. Debates have quickly arisen over the interpretation of the spectral data, particularly the recent MIRI observation where ...

Tags: Reviewer, Too: Astrobiology, Reviewer, Too: Exoplanets

Alert: Jacob Lustig-Yaeger (author alert)

entry.title: POT1 mutations predispose to familial melanoma
entry.content:
Authors: GW Snowden, NG Martin, JZ Liu, J Choi, M Makowski...

Abstract: POT1 mutations predispose to familial melanoma Carla Daniela Robles-Espinoza1‡, Mark Harland2‡, Andrew J. Ramsay3‡, Lauren G. Aoude4‡, Víctor Quesada3, Zhihao Ding1, Karen A. Pooley5, Antonia L. Pritchard4, Jessamy C. Tiffen1, Mia Petljak1 ...

Alert: Nick Martin (author alert)

entry.title: THERMAL AND RADIATIVE PROPERTIES OF EXOPLANET ATMOSPHERES ACROSS THE MASS RANGE ANJALI AURÉLIE ALISON PIETTE UNDER THE ...
entry.content:
Authors: N MADHUSUDHAN

Abstract: THERMAL AND RADIATIVE PROPERTIES OF EXOPLANET ATMOSPHERES ACROSS THE MASS RANGE ANJALI AURÉLIE ALISON PIETTE UNDER THE SUPERVISION OF PROF. NIKKU MADHUSUDHAN This thesis is submitted for the ...

Tags: Reviewer, Too: Astrobiology, Reviewer, Too: Exoplanets

Alert: Nikku Madhusudhan (author alert)

entry.title: Scaling laws in empirical networks
entry.content:
Authors: U Dutta, A Ray, A Clauset - arXiv preprint arXiv:2603.20973, 2026

Abstract: How does the shape of a network change as its size increases? Although random graph models provide some expectations for such" scaling behaviors" in the structure of networks, relatively little is known about how empirical network structure scales ...

Tags: Reviewer, too: Complexity

Alert: Aaron Clauset (author alert)

entry.title: Retrieving the Red Edge on Earth-like Planets with Heterogeneous Clouds and Surfaces
entry.content:
Authors: Z Burr, M Damiano, V Kofman, R Hu, GL Villanueva - arXiv preprint arXiv:2603.20033, 2026

Abstract: The detection and characterization of potentially habitable exoplanets is one of the chief goals of astrophysics for the coming decades. Imaging in reflected light is well suited for characterizing Earth-like planets, as much can be learned about these ...

Tags: Reviewer, Too: Astrobiology, Reviewer, Too: Exoplanets

Alert: Renyu Hu (author alert)

entry.title: ExoReLR: Modeling and retrieving exoplanet reflected-light and thermal-emission spectra
entry.content:
Authors: M Damiano, R Hu - Astrophysics Source Code Library, 2026

Abstract: ExoReLR models exoplanet reflected-light and thermal-emission spectra using physics-based forward calculations and Bayesian retrieval for planets ranging from cold giants to sub-Neptunes and terrestrial worlds. It couples radiative transfer with ...

Tags: Reviewer, Too: Astrobiology, Reviewer, Too: Exoplanets

Alert: Renyu Hu (author alert)

entry.title: IntegriLAB: A Blockchain-Enabled Electronic Lab Notebook for Reproducible Neuroimaging Research
entry.content:
Authors: R Easmin, M Veronese, P Allen, G Nordio... - Frontiers in Neuroinformatics

Abstract: In the era of data-intensive science, managing and verifying research processes that include raw data, analysis scripts, workflows, and documentation, remains a major challenge, particularly in complex fields like neuroimaging were multiple processing ...

Alert: Federico Turkheimer (author alert)

entry.title: A high-coverage Neandertal genome from the Altai Mountains reveals population structure among Neandertals
entry.content:
Authors: D Massilani, S Peyrégne, LNM Iasi, C de Filippo... - Proceedings of the National ..., 2026

Abstract: We present a genome sequenced to~ 37-fold genomic coverage from an approximately 110,000-y-old male Neandertal from Denisova Cave in the Altai Mountains and analyze it together with previously published Neandertal genomes of ...

Alert: Tom Higham (author alert)

entry.title: Should I Stay or Should I Go: Comparing MCMC vs. RFR For Modeling Low Temperature Brown Dwarf Spectra from JWST
entry.content:
Authors: E Softich, A Burgasser, A Lueber, J Haynes - American Astronomical Society Meeting ..., 2026

Abstract: Brown dwarf spectra are challenging to model as their cold temperatures result in strong molecular features, complicated chemistry and even condensate clouds. Models have had to increase in complexity to match improved data, and are thus ...

Tags: Reviewer, Too: Exoplanets

Alert: Adam J. Burgasser (author alert)

entry.title: pySpextool: A Python-Based Data Reduction Package for SpeX on the NASA IRTF
entry.content:
Authors: M Cushing, A Burgasser, K Cruz, C Theissen, CC Hsu - ... Society Meeting Abstracts, 2026

Abstract: We present pySpextool, a python-based data reduction package for the near-infrared spectrograph SpeX on the NASA Infrared Telescope Facility. The algorithms are based on those included in the GUI-based reduction package written in the ...

Tags: Reviewer, Too: Exoplanets

Alert: Adam J. Burgasser (author alert)

entry.title: Mg2+ Catalyzes Nonenzymatic RNA Primer Extension through a Concerted Outer-Sphere Mechanism
entry.content:
Authors: R Wang, C Nisler, Q Cui, L Gagliardi, JW Szostak

Journal: 2026

Abstract: The nonenzymatic replication of the primordial genetic material was an essential stage in the origin of life. One intensively studied model for this process is the template-directed extension of an RNA primer with imidazolium-bridged dinucleotide ...

Tags: Reviewer, Too: Origin of Life

Alert: Jack W. Szostak (author alert)
